chapter_title.ar: بَابٌ فِي أُصُولِ الْكُفْرِ وَ أَرْكَانِهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 115 – The origins of the disbelief and its corner-stones

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (10/76)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: ضعيف - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (10/76)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) رَجُلٌ عَلَى هَذَا الْأَمْرِ إِنْ حَدَّثَ كَذَبَ وَ إِنْ وَعَدَ أَخْلَفَ وَ إِنِ ائْتُمِنَ خَانَ مَا مَنْزِلَتُهُ قَالَ هِيَ أَدْنَى الْمَنَازِلِ مِنَ الْكُفْرِ وَ لَيْسَ بِكَافِرٍ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from hasan Bin Atiyya, from Yazeed Al Sa’ig who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullahasws, ‘A man of this matter (Al-Willayah), if he narrates he lies, and if he promises he breaks, and if he is entrusted he betrays. What is his status?’ Heasws said: ‘It is the closest of the levels from the disbelief, but he is not a disbeliever’.
en.sarwar: 5. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Hassan ibn ‘Atiyyah from Yazid al-Sa’igh who has said the following: “Once I said to abu ‘Abd Allah (a.s.) ‘A man who is in this cause (belief of Shi’a Muslims), but the words he speaks are lies, disregards his promise, and violates his trust. What is his value?’ The Imam said, ‘It is very close to disbelief but he is not an unbeliever.’”
